propositions: The release workflow's only publish gate checks for a v* tag push and never verifies the tagged SHA is an ancestor of reviewed main, allowing an arbitrary pushed tag to publish.
Re-key the release trigger to workflow_dispatch from main so the published source is reviewed by construction, and remove the push:tags publish trigger.
Add a version-lockstep check that asserts the dispatch input equals pyproject.toml, server.json top-level, and all server.json packages[].version before build.
Add an ancestry assertion via git merge-base --is-ancestor with fetch-depth:0 as defense-in-depth against non-main HEADs.

Re-key the pypi deployment environment from v*-tags-only to main-via-dispatch with required reviewers, and add a release-time preflight that queries the GitHub environments API and fails closed if reviewers or main-only branch policy are missing.
Create the v* tag and GitHub Release only after a successful OIDC direct publish (tag as output, never a trigger), and unit-test the extracted guard helpers with fixtures.
